title: STANDARD FOR BUTTER CXS 279-1971 Formerly CODEX STAN A-1-1971. Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2003, 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR BUTTER CXS 279-1971 Formerly CODEX STAN A-1-1971. Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2003, 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.
sections.summary: Document title and revision history: "Standard for Butter" (CXS 279-1971), adopted 1971 and revised/amended in 1999, 2003, 2006, 2010, 2018 and 2022.

Records the 2022 amendment made at the 45th Codex session which replaces prior non-retail container labelling text with a requirement to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
States the standard applies to butter intended for direct consumption or for further processing as described in Section 2.
Defines butter as a fatty product derived exclusively from milk and/or milk-derived products, primarily in the form of a water‑in‑oil emulsion.

Introductory section setting out essential composition and quality factors for the product, specifying milk/milk-derived raw materials, permitted ingredients (sodium chloride/food-grade salt, harmless lactic/flavour-producing starter cultures, potable water) and composition limits including minimum milkfat 80% m/m, maximum water 16% m/m and maximum milk solids-not-fat 2% m/m.
Defines raw materials for the product as milk and/or products obtained from milk.
Lists permitted ingredients: sodium chloride/food-grade salt, starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/or flavour-producing bacteria, and potable water.
Specifies compositional requirements for the product: minimum milkfat 80% m/m, maximum water 16% m/m, and maximum milk solids-not-fat 2% m/m.
Requires a minimum milkfat content of 80% m/m.
Sets a maximum water content of 16% m/m.
Sets a maximum milk solids-not-fat content of 2% m/m.
Permits use of food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for Food Category 02.2.1 (Butter) in foods subject to this standard.

Requires that products covered by the standard comply with maximum levels for contaminants and toxins in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995), and that milk used also meet the contaminant/toxin limits and the CAC-established maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides for milk.

Recommends that products be prepared and handled in accordance with Codex hygiene texts (General Principles of Food Hygiene CXC 1-1969, Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products CXC 57-2004) and any applicable microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Introductory labelling provision stating that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), specific additional labelling provisions in this standard apply.
Specifies the legal name: the food shall be named 'Butter', and the term 'butter' with a suitable qualification shall be used for butter with more than 95% fat.

sections.section: 7.1.1 Butter may be labelled to indicate whether it is salted or unsalted according to national legislation.
sections.summary: Permits butter to be labelled as salted or unsalted in accordance with national legislation.

Requires declaration of milkfat content when omission would mislead consumers, either as a percentage by mass or in grams per serving (with the number of servings stated), in a manner acceptable in the country of sale.
States that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Requires use of the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions of this standard when checking compliance.
